retry_reason: Allocation of kzalloc may fail due to temporary memory pressure; retrying after freeing memory could succeed.
contract_summary: Set-wrapper that allocates a new wrapper_msg, initializes its kref to 1 (establishing the initial reference), adds it to the wrappers list, and returns the object to the caller. The caller owns the initial reference (refcount=1) and must later call kref_put (or a corresponding release function) to free the object when done. On memory allocation failure, returns NULL; the caller MUST check the return value for NULL and may retry if memory becomes available.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate and initialize a refcounted wrapper_msg object, set its refcount to 1 via kref_init, and add it to a list. The only other actions are trivial (allocation, list insertion, pointer assignment) and directly serve the object lifecycle; the refcount set is the core semantic.

check.reason: kref_init initializes w->ref_count to 1, setting the refcount on the returned object. The access path and type chain match the callee information and the code.
